scope.summary: Extend Alembic export (MT-252) to write named morph-weight channel tracks as AbcGeom::IFloatGeomParam channels alongside the cloth mesh cache, and implement Alembic morph-channel import so avatar morph animation can drive cloth re-drape over the timeline.
acceptance_criteria: Alembic export writes one named float channel per active morph target weight track alongside the cloth mesh OPoints sample.
Alembic import reads named float channels from an imported .abc file and constructs a MorphTrack in the animation timeline.
A round-trip (export with morph channels, re-import) preserves channel names and sampled values within float32 tolerance.
The sim-over-timeline pass (MT-289) reads the interpolated morph weight at each frame and applies it to the avatar body shape before the cloth solve step.
scope.risk_if_missed: Alembic export is point-cache only, losing morph channel animation. Downstream DCC tools (Maya, Houdini) cannot reconstruct the morph-driven cloth sequence. MD 2026.0 Alembic carries morph channel data; Tailor's Alembic would be a degraded format for any pipeline using blend shapes.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
